steps.title: Find the sample proportion
steps.content: In Scott"s random sample of 20 employees, 16 are enrolled in exactly three professional development courses. The proportion is $$\frac{16}{20}=0.8.$$

steps.title: Scale to the full population
steps.content: Multiply the sample proportion by the total 400 employees: $$0.8\times 400=320.$$ So the best estimate is 320 employees.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 4 — this is far too small; 4 is the number not enrolled in the sample, not a scaled estimate. Eliminate.
  • B) 320 — matches $0.8\times 400=320$. Keep.
  • C) 380 — would require a sample proportion of $380/400=0.95$, not $0.8$. Eliminate.
  • D) 384 — would require a sample proportion of $384/400=0.96$, not $0.8$. Eliminate.
That confirms B is correct.
